chapter_records.title: ASI Is a Stack, Not a Model
chapter_records.reader_stakes: Start with the architecture's basic contract: the subject is not a magic model and not a loose pile of agent tricks. It is a governed system whose parts must say what they own, what they may do, what evidence they create, and how they can be stopped or replaced.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: The first claim of the book is modest but load-bearing: ASI should be read as a stack. A model can generate, compress, criticize, and propose, but a stack decides when those outputs become plans, claims, permissions, artifacts, or evidence. That separation is what makes later governance possible.

key_figures.caption: Draft ASI Stack control plane.
key_figures.alt_text: Draft ASI Stack control plane figure showing inputs, governed stack layers, an authority rail, an evidence rail, and an improvement loop. The figure frames the architecture as accountable handoffs among intent, planning, memory, claims, execution, routing, evidence, release records, residuals, and rollback paths.
key_figures.non_claim_boundary: Reader figure does not establish implementation, evidence adequacy, safety, readiness, AGI, or ASI.

chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: The Efficient ASI Hypothesis
chapter_records.reader_stakes: The stack is now defined, but it cannot remain only a governance diagram. A good architecture should spend high-cost cognition where the contract demands it, reuse prior work when it remains valid, and preserve enough evidence that shortcuts do not become hidden errors.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: The efficient path to advanced capability is an economy of cognition. Capability grows when work can move to the smallest adequate capability, solved work can be reused, lawful structure can be compressed without hiding repair cost, and fallback routes remain available when the cheap path is not enough.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: System Boundaries and Authority
chapter_records.reader_stakes: The stack now has an identity and an efficiency claim to govern the work ahead. The authority boundary prevents efficiency from turning into ambient permission. A planner can produce a useful plan, a model can produce a plausible answer, and a memory system can surface relevant context, but none of those facts should automatically authorize external action.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Governance becomes operational only when authority is a transition system rather than a mood. A layer may propose, read, transform, execute, approve, or deploy only when the matching grant exists and remains within the active ceiling. Everything else is a denial, escalation, or missing-authority record.

key_figures.caption: Draft authority-to-effect path.
key_figures.alt_text: Draft authority-to-effect path figure showing a requester, permission-class check, authority ceiling, scoped approval, authorized adapter handoff, external effect, effect receipt, denial or escalation path, rollback, and incident handling.
key_figures.non_claim_boundary: Reader figure does not establish implementation, evidence adequacy, safety, readiness, AGI, or ASI.

chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.

chapter_records.title: Failure Modes of Ungoverned Intelligence
chapter_records.reader_stakes: Once the architecture has named boundaries and authority, it has to say what happens when those boundaries fail. The failure model is not a catalog of dramatic disaster scenarios. It is a way to make failures operational: a broken contract should leave a receipt, owner, containment route, and residual question.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Failure Modes of Ungoverned Intelligence gives the stack its obligation map. Each failure names a boundary that must detect, contain, and preserve evidence: authority for overreach, VCM for polluted context, verification for false certainty, execution controls for side effects, and evidence ledgers for residuals and negative results. The external comparator set now ties the chapter's internal failure names to accident-risk, goal-misgeneralization, learned-optimization, power-seeking, and Goodhart/proxy-failure literature.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Evidence States and Claim Discipline
chapter_records.reader_stakes: After naming failure modes, the architecture needs a discipline for how strong each sentence is allowed to sound. The evidence layer makes a simple promise: a claim's wording, label, and support state should not drift apart.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Evidence States and Claim Discipline is the book's anti-inflation layer and one half of its methodological contribution with Living Book Methodology. It lets the manuscript be ambitious without pretending that design rationales, source notes, schemas, Lean proofs, benchmarks, and empirical results are the same thing.

key_figures.caption: Draft evidence-state ladder.
key_figures.alt_text: Draft evidence-state ladder figure separating claim labels from support states and showing transition review, promotion, no change, demotion or refutation, blockers, residuals, non-claims, and public ledger updates.

chapter_records.title: Scalable Oversight and Adversarial AI Control
chapter_records.worked_example_ref: line:182:Worked trace: a selectively reported update
chapter_records.reader_stakes: Oversight is not simply finding someone willing to review a system. It is deciding what that reviewer can actually know. A specialist may have less time than the system had to produce an answer. A human may not see the same evidence. A group of models may sound independent while sharing the same blind spot. An argument can be persuasive without being right.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Scalable oversight is an interface problem between limited supervisors and systems whose work may be difficult to inspect. A governed stack handles that problem with a protocol record: scope, access, roles, incentives, baseline, outcome audit, correlation risk, residual, and accountable escalation. It can use assistance to surface questions and counterarguments without treating an agent role or a polished transcript as a proof.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Human Intent as a Formal Input
chapter_records.reader_stakes: The authority frame now has to meet a real request without turning eagerness into permission. The operational path from human purpose into the stack begins here. It asks how a request becomes a contract without letting ambiguity become permission.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Human Intent as a Formal Input is the stack's entry adapter. It turns natural-language purpose into a bounded artifact that planning, alignment, execution, and evidence can inspect.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Constitutional Alignment: Agency, Dignity, and Corrigibility
chapter_records.reader_stakes: When a powerful system acts consequentially, people need more than a statement that it values them. They need working handles. They need to refuse, inspect, appeal, correct, exit, recover, and identify who is accountable while the system is still in a position to change course.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Constitutional alignment becomes meaningful when protected commitments can change what the system is allowed to do. Agency and dignity become meaningful when affected people still have usable correction paths at the moment power is requested. Corrigibility becomes meaningful when those paths remain available after deployment pressure, memory updates, automation, and capability replacement make correction inconvenient.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Moral Uncertainty, Value Conflict, and Contestable Governance
chapter_records.reader_stakes: The hardest governance cases are not the ones where everyone agrees. They are the cases where a system must act while values conflict, affected parties disagree, and no honest objective can make the disagreement disappear.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Moral uncertainty becomes governable when unresolved obligations stay visible after action. Governance rights become meaningful when affected people retain usable handles to audit, appeal, leave, fork where safe, and preserve dissent while the system is still capable of changing course.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Stable Capability Fields
chapter_records.reader_stakes: Governance rights ask what must survive institutional change across long time horizons. Stable Capability Fields ask what must survive technical change. If a capability is replaced, the surrounding system needs to know what identity, authority, evidence, and rollback obligations stayed stable. A capability name is a promise only if the boundary behind the name is conserved.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Stable Capability Fields are upgrade sockets for governed intelligence. They let a capability improve by changing implementations while preserving semantic identity, authority ceilings, evaluator policy, evidence, route validity, and rollback.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Capability Replacement and Rollback
chapter_records.reader_stakes: Stable Capability Fields define the boundary that replacement must cross under review. Replacement is the transaction that crosses it. It is not swapping one component for another; it is a controlled change with compatibility checks, canary scope, monitoring, rollback, and residual ownership. The field says what must remain true; the transaction says whether the candidate earned the right to become ordinary infrastructure.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Capability Replacement and Rollback turns improvement into a transaction. It preserves the prior state, candidate evidence, regressions, residuals, approval, monitor window, and rollback path.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Security Kernel and Digital SCIFs
chapter_records.reader_stakes: Replacement and rollback protect the shape of a capability under change. Security protects the powers and secrets that capability can touch. As the stack grows more useful, context, credentials, tools, and private records become part of the safety problem. A replacement that changes what it can see or do has changed the security boundary, even if its public interface still looks familiar.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: The security kernel is the least-exposure boundary for secrets and privileged context. The core design is conservative and practical: do not put secrets and privileged context where the model can freely transform them. Put them behind handles, clearance checks, ephemeral compartments, and audit receipts. The current synthetic harnesses now exercise the receipt shape for handle substitution, expired approvals, overbroad context, sanitized residuals, prompt-injection non-disclosure, and security-overhead budget-laundering rejection. The next maturity step is a stricter threat model for logs, summaries, side channels, approval expiry, and runtime budget enforcement, followed by replayed or deployed runtime traces.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Model-Weight Custody and Hardware Roots of Trust
chapter_records.worked_example_ref: line:162:Worked custody trace: a valid token for the wrong artifact
chapter_records.reader_stakes: A model's weights are the artifact that makes a particular trained capability portable. They can be copied into backups, deployment images, and recipient environments before anyone decides what each copy means. A system may correctly refuse a tool call while the model artifact that produces the request has already escaped its declared boundary.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Model weights are not merely files to load. They are a high-value, mutable artifact whose identity, custody, access, environment, release scope, incident path, and irreversibility affect what a governed system can honestly say about its boundary. The practical discipline is to make these states explicit before key release or loading, and to keep an invalid or stale condition from being silently bypassed.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: AI Supply-Chain Integrity and Lifecycle Provenance
chapter_records.reader_stakes: A modern AI system rarely arrives as one sealed program today. It is assembled from a base model, datasets, tokenizers, code libraries, build and training jobs, cloud services, policy files, and derivatives. A familiar name does not reveal which ingredient changed, who supplied it, or whether an upstream warning still applies.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: AI supply-chain integrity gives a system a disciplined answer to a practical question: what exactly are we relying on when we rely on this artifact? The answer names more than a model file. It includes the data, code, dependencies, build or training record, signer, supplier, advisory state, derivative chain, release scope, and retirement conditions that remain relevant to a bounded decision.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Recursive Self-Improvement Boundaries
chapter_records.reader_stakes: Bounded self-improvement is the hardest promise in the stack: the system may improve itself, but it may not become its own unchecked source of permission, evidence, or moral authority. Intent contracts, protected constraints, rights, stable fields, rollback, and security boundaries become the gate that any powerful operational layer must pass. A stronger system should inherit tighter receipts, not looser ones.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Recursive self-improvement is allowed only through stable fields, replacement transactions, independent evaluators, protected invariants, governance approval, monitoring, and rollback. Improvement is not a special exemption from the stack's rules; it is the hardest case for those rules.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Open-Ended Improvement Engines
chapter_records.worked_example_ref: line:161:Worked campaign trace: the faster agent that loses its stop boundary
chapter_records.reader_stakes: An improving system needs both a workshop and a release gate. The workshop may search for programs, tools, tests, or designs, compare alternatives, and retain useful failures. The release gate decides whether any result may change a live system. Treating the workshop's own score as that decision makes a search loop easy to fool with a flattering task or proxy.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Open-ended improvement needs a control plane of its own. It is not enough to govern the final change if the process that generated the change has hidden its task policy, evaluator limits, rejected alternatives, resource cost, or stop conditions. A campaign record makes that process reviewable without pretending that every search is dangerous or that every discovery is a capability advance.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Command Contracts: From Intent to Executable Work
chapter_records.reader_stakes: A person can ask for a complicated thing in ordinary language, with examples, hopes, limits, and uncertainty. A system should not treat that ordinary language as unlimited permission.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: The merged destination chapter makes executable work depend on explicit semantic contracts rather than conversational momentum. A request can begin in ordinary language, but tool use, runtime dispatch, artifact delivery, and evidence updates need a contract object that preserves meaning, authority, and failure behavior.

chapter_records.title: Planning as a Control Layer: DAGs and Intelligence Arbitrage
chapter_records.reader_stakes: Once a command is structured, the stack has to decide what work should exist and what kind of intelligence each piece deserves. Planning turns intent into obligations: dependencies, budgets, context needs, capability tiers, route choices, adequacy contracts, review points, and stop conditions.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Planning converts accepted commands into governed structure. It exposes assumptions, tasks, dependencies, context requests, tools, authority, risk, budgets, capability tiers, adequacy contracts, verification, stop conditions, and replanning policy before execution begins.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Cognitive Compilation and Semantic IR
chapter_records.reader_stakes: A scheduled plan is still too loose unless each unit of work has an artifact language. Cognitive compilation gives the stack that language: semantic atoms, obligations, validators, repairs, and target outputs that can be inspected before they become code, prose, schemas, proofs, or tool jobs.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Cognitive Compilation and Semantic IR gives the stack a compiler boundary between intention and artifact. It asks the system to stop treating generation as one opaque act and instead lower goals into semantic atoms, obligations, validators, repair locations, and target backends that can be scheduled and audited.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: The Virtual Context ABI: Typed Pages, Cells, and Certificates
chapter_records.reader_stakes: A model can sound as if it remembers everything across a long conversation. A governed system has to be able to say what it actually gave the model.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: The Virtual Context ABI is the static interface between durable memory and model-visible context. It gives context a stable identity before the stack uses it. A context packet should say where it came from, which version it represents, what type of cell it is, what was omitted, which authority survived, which uses are permitted, whether the packet is admitted or adequate, and which fault or residual remains.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Context Transactions, Snapshots, Mounts, and Taint
chapter_records.reader_stakes: Typed context cells still need runtime semantics before use in memory. Context becomes a transactional view: it can be mounted, branched, tainted, committed, rolled back, expired, declassified, or denied. Without those semantics, even well-labeled memory can become stale, mixed across incompatible tasks, or derived from material that should no longer be used.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Context transactions, snapshots, mounts, and taint give VCM runtime semantics. Memory becomes immutable events, versioned pages, coherent snapshots, mounted views, copy-on-write branches, tainted derivatives, deletion obligations, contradiction records, and auditable faults.

key_figures.caption: Draft context transaction lifecycle.
key_figures.alt_text: Draft context transaction lifecycle figure showing context ABI records, certificates, transaction records, snapshots, mount policies, branch or commit decisions, taint and deletion closure, materialized views, faults, quarantine, downstream artifact, adequacy, claim-ledger, and job handoff boundaries.

chapter_records.title: Verification Bandwidth and Context Adequacy
chapter_records.reader_stakes: A coherent memory view can still be insufficient for verification work. The stack has to separate having context from being able to verify with it. It needs to know when the available context, attention, time, tools, comparison method, and reviewer capacity are adequate for the claim being made.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Verification Bandwidth and Context Adequacy is the stack's refusal to equate access with understanding. A system may hold the right documents, have enough context length, and still lack the time, comparison method, tool support, or reviewer bandwidth needed to verify the claim it is about to make.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Claim Ledgers and Belief Revision
chapter_records.reader_stakes: Once verification adequacy is visible, claims need a durable revision home. Important statements become ledgered objects that can be supported, challenged, downgraded, split, merged, promoted, or retired without losing their identity.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Claim Ledgers and Belief Revision makes belief state durable. Claims are separated from prose, tied to evidence and uncertainty, linked to contradictions, and revised without overwriting history. The sources support the need for a ledgered boundary, while the extractor, contradiction detector, and revision engine remain implementation work this repository has not yet done.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Proof-Carrying Claims and Adversarial Review
chapter_records.reader_stakes: Some claims are cheap enough to remain drafts while evidence matures. Other claims shape plans, permissions, releases, or safety boundaries. Those claims need more than a well-written paragraph.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Proof-carrying claims and adversarial review are one verification surface. The claim ledger says what the claim is and how its belief state changes. This verification-event layer says what kind of proof, citation, procedure, replay, benchmark, tribunal, or human-review record is adequate before a selected claim or artifact can ask for stronger standing.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Labor OS and Typed Jobs
chapter_records.reader_stakes: After planning, verification, and review, the stack has to turn accepted work into bounded execution. A Labor OS is the layer where tasks become typed jobs with lifecycle, parentage, inputs, outputs, approvals, isolation, logs, failure behavior, and completion criteria.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Labor OS and Typed Jobs turns plans into controlled work. It gives each executable unit a type, contract, lifecycle, permissions, approval state, adapter, artifacts, failure behavior, audit events, and replay status. The sources justify treating execution as typed work, but this repo still lacks the scheduler, permission service, approval service, adapter runner, and replay system that would turn the boundary into runtime evidence.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Artifact Graphs, Audit Logs, and Replay
chapter_records.reader_stakes: Typed jobs become trustworthy when their outputs remain traceable after action. Artifact graphs make those outputs durable: artifacts, logs, traces, receipts, replay records, source links, context links, and claim or test links. Without that graph, work disappears into conversation history.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Artifact Graphs, Audit Logs, and Replay turns outputs into durable work products. Each artifact should carry identity, parent job, source/context/tool links, claim/test links, audit events, canonical/projection state, reverse dependencies, replay classification, terminal completion, relocation identity, environment assumptions, provenance status, and replay limits. Original architecture sources remain passage-reviewed or connector-bounded, while the five historical projects are treated as one local lineage whose failures sharpen the graph without becoming independent runtime evidence.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Runtime Adapters, Tool Permissions, and Human Approval
chapter_records.reader_stakes: Artifact graphs record work, but runtime adapters create external effects under authority. The adapter is the boundary where a typed job may touch tools, files, networks, deployments, payments, credentials, hardware, or external systems under permission and approval.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Runtime adapters, tool permissions, and human approval form the effects boundary. They map jobs to adapters, check permissions, mediate authority handles, enforce approval gates, invoke tools in bounded modes, record effect receipts, and capture rollback or residuals.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Inter-Stack Protocols, Identity, and Economic Exchange
chapter_records.worked_example_ref: line:159:Worked trace: the translated analysis with a disputed receipt
chapter_records.reader_stakes: Intelligent systems will not remain one program on one machine for long. A planner may ask a remote specialist for analysis, an agent may ask a tool for an effect, a service may return an artifact, and an organization may reserve budget for work that takes place across several operators. Each handoff raises consequential questions: Who asked? Who is receiving the request? What exactly was requested? Whose authority is being used? When does that authority expire? What did the other side actually return? Who owns the remaining uncertainty when it fails?
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Cross-stack interaction is a governed handoff, not a message-shaped permission. The exchange contract keeps protocol structure, identity, credential verification, delegated authority, economic commitment, result evidence, revocation, dispute, and residual ownership distinct until existing local owners make their bounded decisions. It gives a remote request a durable local meaning without pretending that a common protocol eliminates independent judgment.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Procedural Memory and Cognitive Loop Closure
chapter_records.reader_stakes: Repeated work creates a question the execution layer cannot avoid: what should the stack learn from traces that keep recurring? Procedural memory is the path from comparable traces to a reusable tool, checklist, workflow, or capability candidate, after the evidence and boundaries are mature enough.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Procedural Memory and Cognitive Loop Closure turns repeated traces into governed tools. It detects loops, abstracts invariant structure, discovers parameters, synthesizes candidate tools, verifies them, registers them, monitors them, and retires or revises them when they fail. The present evidence boundary is still a record-gate boundary: the sources and mappings explain what such closure requires, Lean checks finite generated-tool, regression, lifecycle-route, and fixture-bridge gates, and the synthetic harness checks procedure-qualification packet discipline, while deployed tool generation, regression-quality testing, routing, monitoring, retirement automation, and a full loop-closure run remain unexecuted.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Routing Heads and Specialist Cores
chapter_records.reader_stakes: Repeated work can become procedural memory records only after evidence matures. That creates the operational routing question: which bounded capability should handle the next piece of work, and why is it allowed to do so?
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Routing makes modular intelligence governable only when it is recorded as a bounded decision. The head selects; the specialist acts within a local envelope; evidence updates readiness; residuals become future pressure. The folded MoECOT material adds the runtime packet shape that a concrete orchestrator would need to emit. The present evidence stops before route quality and runtime behavior: the sources, fixtures, Lean predicates, readiness/residual harness, and route-lease harness define the decision surface, while local router runs, specialist-selection benchmarks, MoECOT replay packets, residual-routing traces, and safety evaluations remain future work.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Readiness Gates, Residual Escrow, and Quarantine
chapter_records.reader_stakes: Routing can identify a plausible specialist, but plausibility is not readiness. The readiness gate asks when a route, model, tool, memory packet, procedure, runtime lane, or replacement is allowed to become ordinary execution and when it must stay in shadow, canary, escrow, quarantine, diagnostic use, or review. The gate is where evidence becomes permission, and where missing evidence becomes an explicit operating mode instead of a hidden exception.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Readiness is the stack's refusal to confuse availability with qualification. Gates decide when a module can move from experiment to route, residual escrow keeps unsolved cases alive, and quarantine turns failure into governed learning rather than hidden state.

key_figures.caption: Draft readiness residual quarantine map.
key_figures.alt_text: Draft readiness residual quarantine map showing a candidate route or replacement proposal entering a readiness gate record, splitting into limited allowed use, residual escrow, quarantine, blocker handling, review rerun, gate-laundering boundary, and support-state non-claim boundary.

chapter_records.title: Personal Compute Hives and Federated Edge Intelligence
chapter_records.reader_stakes: Runtime orchestration asks what an orchestrator should emit for review before placement. The placement problem follows immediately: where can such orchestration safely live? A personal compute hive is not every device the system can reach; it is a governed federation of portals, workers, storage, rented nodes, family surfaces, and project machines.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Personal Compute Hives make the ASI Stack materially personal. They give the architecture an owned substrate without pretending that every reachable machine is safe to use. The design center is simple: compile work into contracts, represent devices as bounded resources, reject unlawful nodes before optimizing, and record why execution happened where it happened. A complete implementation would let a person, family, or project use local and rented compute while keeping identity, privacy, authority, evidence, and revocation visible.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Compact Generative Systems: Generate, Verify, Repair, and Residual Honesty
chapter_records.reader_stakes: Once the stack has routed work onto governed compute, compactness becomes tempting. Smaller seeds, summaries, procedures, generators, and generated reconstructions matter only when the remaining burden is honest.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Compact systems are useful when they generate, reconstruct, or represent more value than their hidden burdens cost. Residual-honest accounting therefore keeps seed, rule system, memory, generation, verification, repair, semantic grounding, residuals, governance, fallback, consumer policy, and evidence in the same record.

key_figures.caption: Draft compression and generation acceptance.

chapter_records.title: Fast Generation Architectures
chapter_records.reader_stakes: Compression is not the only way speed matters in generation pipelines. The generation substrate itself has to be part of the architecture: how much planning, verification, routing, tool use, and repair can fit inside a fixed latency and compute budget. A faster decoder can help the stack, but it can also make weak plans, stale context, or skipped checks fail faster.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Fast Generation Architectures defines the ASI Stack's generation substrate. Its thesis is not "make tokens faster." Its thesis is that generation speed becomes useful only when the stack can select a mode, verify accepted output, count repair and fallback cost, and promote the mode through evidence rather than excitement.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Governed Deliberation and Test-Time Scaling
chapter_records.worked_example_ref: line:142:Worked trace: the polished patch that lost the correct first answer
chapter_records.reader_stakes: Some requests deserve an immediate answer with uncertainty visible up front. Others deserve careful checks and permission to stop. An intelligent system needs to know the difference. Spending more computation can produce alternative plans, catch an obvious error, or explore a difficult problem. It can also produce a longer, more convincing mistake.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Deliberation is valuable when it creates a better bounded candidate, not when it creates an aura of thoughtfulness. A governed architecture needs a place to decide when extra inference is worth spending and when a result must remain a draft, a residual, or a request for review. That decision is neither fast decoding nor planning nor execution.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: RankFold, NeuralFold, and Artifact Compression
chapter_records.reader_stakes: After fast generation, the stack returns to stored artifacts and retrieval. RankFold and NeuralFold are treated as compression-architecture ideas: ways to represent large artifacts more cheaply while preserving routes back to the full object when needed.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Artifact compression is a routing decision under evidence. A compressed artifact may be cheaper to store, but it becomes usable only when reconstruction, residuals, probes, fallback, utility, and cost accounting are explicit.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Resource Economics and Token Budgets
chapter_records.reader_stakes: Governed routing, compression, faster generation, richer representations, and synthetic experiments can look locally cheap. Resource economics asks whether they still save anything after verification, repair, fallback, memory pressure, fidelity limits, and risk are counted.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Resources are part of governance. A system that cannot account for tokens, verification, latency, human review, risk, fidelity, transfer limits, and quality cannot honestly claim efficiency.

key_figures.caption: Draft route-selection budget tradeoff.
key_figures.alt_text: Draft route-selection budget tradeoff figure showing a task request, eligibility gates, selected bounded transform plus verifier route at 14.2 cost units, adequate overkill baseline at 43.0 cost units, rejected failed-verification route at 2.3 cost units, rejected hidden-residual route at 8.2 cost units, and no deployed-router or core-claim-promotion boundary.

chapter_records.title: Mathematical and Search Substrates
chapter_records.reader_stakes: After bounding simulations, the stack turns to optional mathematical and search substrates. Elegant calculi, geometric search spaces, coils, cyclic compute, and state-space structures may be useful, but elegance alone does not make them part of the stack.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Mathematical substrates are valuable when they become disciplined specialists. They should carry baselines, proof boundaries, falsification conditions, evidence requirements, and readiness states.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Circle Calculus and Proof-Carrying AI Contracts
chapter_records.reader_stakes: Optional substrates need baselines before their structure can travel safely into contracts. Circle Calculus narrows that idea to proof-carrying structural facts: finite claims that can move through the stack with receipt references, deterministic fields, and replay expectations. The target is not grand certainty, but portable restraint. It is small certified structure that other layers can consume without guessing what was checked.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Circle Calculus and proof-carrying AI contracts let finite structural facts travel with theorem IDs, receipts, fingerprints, validation commands, and non-claims. Their job is boundary preservation.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Coil Attention, Cyclic Memory, and Recurrence Contracts
chapter_records.reader_stakes: Proof-carrying receipts become especially important for memory and recurrence, where structural language can sound like semantic understanding. Cyclic memory, sparse coverage, freshness, lag, and recurrence schedules need records before anyone treats them as reasoning quality.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Cyclic memory contracts make structural facts visible: residue, winding, freshness, coverage gaps, recurrence depth, active token work, loop exits, and fallback. They do not prove retrieval or reasoning quality.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: CoilRA, MultiCoil RoPE, and Cyclic Mixers
chapter_records.reader_stakes: The same discipline now moves inside model mechanisms under review: cyclic adapters, phase banks, RoPE variants, route heads, and mixers. These may become useful substrate candidates, but the stack treats them as candidates until baselines, diagnostics, and failure cases exist.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Cyclic structure can be an excellent substrate when the workload actually contains cyclic structure and the evidence boundary is preserved. It is not a general magic advantage.

key_figures.caption: Draft cyclic substrate adoption gate.
key_figures.alt_text: Draft cyclic substrate adoption gate figure showing a cyclic candidate such as RoPE, CoilRA, a circulant mixer, or cyclic memory moving through structural receipts, baseline symmetry, measured tradeoffs, canary routing, and retirement paths.

chapter_records.title: Executable Specifications and Lean Proof Envelope
chapter_records.reader_stakes: Elegant substrates and structural receipts alone do not prove broad capability. The evidence envelope makes that warning operational before claims harden into confidence. Some claims should become schemas, some tests, some Lean predicates, and some should remain open research questions.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: The proof envelope gives the book a disciplined way to use formality without theatrical certainty. It asks every formal-sounding claim to shrink until it becomes a question that can be checked honestly.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Benchmark Ratchets and Anti-Goodhart Evidence
chapter_records.reader_stakes: Executable specs say what a narrow artifact can certify before use. Benchmarks ask how measured performance should change future behavior without becoming a game. Benchmark ratchets can move a floor forward only when regressions, scope, baselines, and negative results remain visible.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Benchmarks are pressure surfaces, not truth machines. They become useful when every result has a run record, baseline, origin class, target-capacity check, inherited floors, retry lineage, checkpoint/output binding, residuals, regressions, anti-Goodhart checks, and a conservative support-state effect.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Capability Thresholds and Deployment Commitments
chapter_records.worked_example_ref: line:142:Worked trace: the crossed cyber threshold with a stale exception
chapter_records.reader_stakes: A threshold earns its place when it changes a decision that was specified before the number arrived. Otherwise it is easy to celebrate a score, explain away an uncomfortable result, and later call the improvisation a policy. The important question is what the line was meant to mean, for which task, under what test conditions, and what the organization had already agreed to do next.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Capability thresholds are most useful when they are promises made before a result is known. The promise says which domain and threat are being assessed, what the test can and cannot show, what uncertainty remains, which safeguards must be verified, who can approve an exception, and which release path is affected. The result then changes only that bounded path.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Adversarial Evaluation, Sandbagging, and Training-Time Deception
chapter_records.worked_example_ref: line:131:Worked trace: the model that improves when the monitor disappears
chapter_records.reader_stakes: An evaluation answers a narrower question than people often want it to answer. It tells us what a system did with a particular prompt, monitor, reward, task distribution, time budget, and consequence. It does not automatically reveal what the system could do in another context or whether the signal used to train it remained faithful to the task.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Evaluation integrity is the discipline of refusing to let a narrow observation become a broader story. It keeps training, testing, monitoring, and promotion separate enough that a useful result can remain useful even when its limits are discovered. The core claim stays at `argument`; source notes, planned fixtures, and formal hooks do not establish local resistance to deception.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Safety Cases and Structured Assurance
chapter_records.worked_example_ref: line:159:Worked trace: a stale control result and an unresolved defeater
chapter_records.reader_stakes: Complex systems can accumulate evidence far faster than they accumulate understanding. There may be test results, model cards, policy documents, proof files, incident notes, and approval records, yet no clear answer to a simple question: what is the argument for allowing this particular system to do this particular thing in this particular setting? A safety case makes that question visible instead of answering it with a folder full of artifacts.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Safety cases make an argument inspectable. They connect a bounded claim to the reasoning, evidence, assumptions, threat model, countercases, and residuals that the claim depends on. That connection is valuable because a complex system's artifacts rarely speak for themselves, especially when their limits and disagreements point in different directions.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Policy Optimization and Learning from Feedback
chapter_records.reader_stakes: Benchmark ratchets create improvement pressure that policy updates must govern carefully. Policy optimization is how that pressure can update planning, routing, context selection, verification, execution, or review behavior without letting reward become authority.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Policy optimization is the learning actuator. Benchmarks and verifiers create pressure, policy optimization proposes behavior changes, and SCF governance decides whether those changes survive.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Data Engines, Continual Learning, and Unlearning
chapter_records.worked_example_ref: line:194:Full-state update contract and worked trace
chapter_records.reader_stakes: Learning systems depend on decisions about what gets to count as experience. Those decisions can disappear inside an import script, replay buffer, or synthetic-example generator, even though they determine which history will influence later behavior and which claims later appear plausible.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: A system that learns without a data lifecycle gradually loses the ability to say why it changed. Provenance, authority, exclusions, contamination controls, retention, deletion scope, evaluation references, and residuals give each learning input a history that can be inspected before it becomes ordinary infrastructure. The point is not to make every candidate impossible to use; it is to give uncertain candidates honest lanes such as quarantine or bounded experimentation.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Artifact Steward Agents and Living Project Governance
chapter_records.reader_stakes: Policy learning changes behavior, but durable projects need continuity across revisions. Artifact stewardship asks how long-lived projects can be cared for without giving an AI steward ownership over the artifact, money, maintainers, or community. The steward is a bounded service layer, not a sovereign.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Artifact Steward Agents extend the living-book discipline from individual chapters to durable projects. They preserve mission, memory, work, evidence, resources, contributors, governance, and sunset criteria without granting the AI ownership. The central rule is that stewardship must make the artifact more inspectable and governable over time. If the steward becomes the only maintainer, the only memory, or the only authority path, it has violated the architecture it was meant to protect.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Integrated Reference Architecture
chapter_records.reader_stakes: After every major layer is named, the machine has to come back together. Intent, planning, memory, routing, verification, execution, evidence, benchmarks, and improvement should cooperate in one inspectable flow.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Integrated Reference Architecture is where the book stops describing layers in isolation and asks whether they compose into one inspectable machine. The reference trace has to carry typed handoffs, authority stops, artifacts, evidence updates, residuals, and self-improvement gates from the first request to the final change record.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Project Theseus as Report-First Implementation Reference
chapter_records.reader_stakes: Integrated architecture remains a design target until Project Theseus can serve as a report-first implementation reference: a way to think about what dashboards, ledgers, configs, gates, and work-board records should look like when a system tries to make itself inspectable.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Project Theseus gives the book a concrete report-first implementation reference: pressure, residuals, plan contracts, gates, ledgers, operator work boards, checkpoints, and self-evolution governance.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Prototype Roadmap
chapter_records.reader_stakes: Project Theseus gives implementation pressure that needs an accountable build order. The roadmap turns that pressure into build order. It starts with evidence hygiene, source ingestion, artifact continuity, and claim discipline before it asks for stronger agency.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: The prototype should grow as evidence infrastructure grows: source matrix, claim ledger, artifact graph, schemas, plans, context, typed jobs, runtime adapters, routing, readiness, benchmarks, SCFs, and only then bounded self-improvement.
chapter_records.divergence_summary: Exact generated Human baseline frozen as initial v2.0 curated source; no additional prose divergence introduced.
meaning_preservation_checks: strongest objection and worked example retained

chapter_records.title: Living Book Methodology
chapter_records.reader_stakes: The prototype roadmap explains how the system should be built over time. The manuscript itself needs the same discipline so it can keep changing without losing its evidence controls. The manuscript is part of the architecture, not a static brochure about it. If the architecture is living, its written form must also have controlled change, reviewable lineage, and a visible boundary between aspiration and evidence.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: Living Book Methodology defines how this project improves without losing its memory. Paired with Evidence States, it is the book's methodological contribution: a claim-aware, source-aware, proof-aware, release-aware way for a technical book to change. The method is not "keep editing until it feels better." It is: ingest sources honestly, revise chapters through stable IDs, track support states, mechanize what can be mechanized, validate the public render, and preserve the residuals.

chapter_records.title: Open Research Agenda and Bibliography Plan
chapter_records.reader_stakes: The research agenda prevents the architecture from hiding its unfinished work after the argument reaches its end. It names what remains missing: sources to load, literature to normalize, experiments to run, proofs to refine, schemas to harden, and claims that must not rise above their current support state. A serious architecture needs that backlog to be public enough that future work cannot quietly skip it.
chapter_records.reader_payoff: The research agenda is the book's backlog discipline. It keeps source ingestion, bibliography, proof targets, experiments, missing evidence, and chapter insertion rules visible so the living book can grow without losing evidence control.
